2604.01204.title: Neural Harmonic Textures for High-Quality Primitive Based Neural Reconstruction
2604.01204.paper_summary: Primitive-based methods such as 3D Gaussian Splatting have recently become the state-of-the-art for novel-view synthesis and related reconstruction tasks. Compared to neural fields, these representations are more flexible, adaptive, and scale better to large scenes. However, the limited expressivity of individual primitives makes modeling high-frequency detail challenging. We introduce Neural Harmonic Textures, a neural representation approach that anchors latent feature vectors on a virtual scaffold surrounding each primitive. These features are interpolated within the primitive at ray intersection points. Inspired by Fourier analysis, we apply periodic activations to the interpolated features, turning alpha blending into a weighted sum of harmonic components. The resulting signal is then decoded in a single deferred pass using a small neural network, significantly reducing computational cost. Neural Harmonic Textures yield state-of-the-art results in real-time novel view synthesis while bridging the gap between primitive- and neural-field-based reconstruction. Our method integrates seamlessly into existing primitive-based pipelines such as 3DGUT, Triangle Splatting, and 2DGS. We further demonstrate its generality with applications to 2D image fitting and semantic reconstruction.
2604.01204.paper_summary_zh: 原始基礎的方法，如 3D 高斯噴濺，最近已成為新視角合成和相關重建任務的最先進技術。與神經場相比，這些表示更具靈活性、適應性，並且在大型場景中擴展性更佳。然後，單個原始物件的有限表現力使得建模高頻細節變得具有挑戰性。我們引入了神經諧波紋理，一種神經表示方法，將潛在特徵向量固定在圍繞每個原始物件的虛擬支架上。這些特徵在光線交點內部進行插值。受到傅里葉分析的啟發，我們對插值特徵應用週期性激活，將 alpha 混合轉化為諧波分量的加權和。然後，使用一個小型神經網絡在單次延遲傳遞中解碼生成的信號，顯著降低了計算成本。神經諧波紋理在實時新視角合成中產生了最先進的結果，同時彌合了基於原始物件和神經場的重建之間的差距。我們的方法無縫整合到現有的基於原始物件的管道中，如 3DGUT、三角形噴濺和 2DGS。我們還通過應用於 2D 圖像擬合和語義重建展示了其通用性。
2604.01204.author: Jorge Condor et.al.
2604.01204.authors: Jorge Condor, Nicolas Moenne-Loccoz, Merlin Nimier-David, Piotr Didyk, Zan Gojcic, Qi Wu
